creator: ivk@kristal.ru
text:
On my FreeBSD server I've mount /dev/ad0s1d partition into /tmp mount point. Permissions set to 1777. Sometimes after clean reboot kernel dumps when trying to mount /tmp. After /sbin/fsck -y /dev/ad0s1d I've reboot and nothing changed. kernel dumps again. Only /sbin/newfs /dev/ad0s1d return my server to work.

How-To-Repeat: I don't know. May be mount some partition into /tmp with permissions 1777 set after. I've use:
- MySQL server, that create /tmp/mysql.sock socket.
- postfix-policyd-weight-0.1.14.17, that create /tmp/.policyd-weight directory
- samba server, that create /tmp/.winbindd directory
